Okay, so this is the first part of the actual sale. Okay, this is titled energy, rapport and belief. All right, and there's three parts of this and all of it is related to your head game. Okay, so I just wanna tell you a story. So Layla was launching a gym in Hawaii and she was crushing it, taking souls and helping people and helping gym owners and all that kind of stuff. But one day she was like a halfway through her day and she was like, dude, something feels off today. Like I sold, I'm five out of 10, so she's a 50% which for her is like an off debt. She's like, oh no, what's going on? Now, I know that Layla was an experienced enough salesperson that it's not, like me telling her what an obstacle overcome is gonna be is not gonna, like that's not gonna solve the problem. It was a head game thing. So I was like, hmm, what happened? So someone asked for like a refund or they changed their mind or whatever and they hit it up. Now normally that shouldn't be on your lines, they're gonna, you route those people to billet. You don't want that in the back of your head because listen, if you sell 200 people, you're gonna have certain people get cold feet. That's anything, it doesn't matter what it is or you're gonna have people get cold feet. It has nothing to do with the product, it has nothing to do with you. It's everything to do with them and their story that they're telling themselves. But on some level, sometimes like if you're not, if you're not experienced, like it can get to you and you don't want that to be in the back of your mind because what your subconscious believes, your conscious mind will act and like the way you say things, the way you phrase them, the energy you have when you're delivering them will change. I guarantee you it will change, okay? So the way that we ended up fixing the problem is that I just read three of the testimonials of people whose lives were absolutely changed and transformed through this program, okay? And then she had signed up like two months prior and I was like, hey, I just wanted to show you these. And she went seven for seven for the rest of the night because she was reminded by how deeply impactful the program that we're selling is. It's an amazing program. And if you haven't sold it yet and you haven't seen someone go through the transformation yet, it's not real for you yet, but I can promise you this program changes people's lives. I've had people lose hundreds of pounds because this was the thing that finally got them to do it. I've had people with 400 pounds, but I haven't been to the gym in 10 straight years. I've never even gone one twice in this program because I was motivated and made me do it. I'm so grateful for you, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. And you get to be that person who connects them, who forces them to make that decision so they can change their lives. You're gonna help them along and help them get over those obstacles that they present for themselves because you're experienced in helping those people make those decisions and helping them come across the finish line, right? So you have to believe in the product. Usually three out of four, any issues that are in sales is there. It's not in the, like, we'll get to the technical stuff of like, oh, I need to think about it. I need to talk to my husband. There's ways to get around that stuff, but the way you're getting around it, with the energy that you get around it, the genuineness that they can see in your eyes is gonna come down to your belief. And if you really, like, put simply, if you believe in this product so much that like, you're like, this will 100% change your life and you will transform your life forever, you should be able to get someone who's not even into the program, who's not even signed up for it, though you're sitting in the grocery line. If you believe in something that much, they should want to sign up because of how amazing they are, right? They should wanna quit wherever they're at or stop whatever they're doing to come and do this program because that's how amazing it is. So like, if you think about it from that perspective, that's how you should be setting the tone for these sales. Like, it's truly amazing and you can lose that. You can get disconnected from it with this repeating bodies, bodies, credit cards, bodies, credit cards, bodies, credit cards, contracts, blah, blah, blah. Like, you can lose that, but you just, if you ever feel like yourself getting in cold streak, remind yourself what you're doing, you're helping these people. And when someone comes in the door, if your objective is to sell them, you will sometimes, a lot of times, if you're really good, but you won't sell as many as if you're looking at them and be like, I'm gonna fucking change this person's life today. I'm going to change her life or I'm going to change his life and I'm gonna help him get out of his own way and really like transform his life. So, belief is huge. It's probably the biggest thing that it's not the sexy one. It's not the like, what's the one liner? What's the zinger? A lot of times it's not one on those. I mean, it sounds sexy, it sounds cool, and it's a cool like meme, but like the war of sales is won or lost on whether who believes more. Like, do you believe in your product more than they believe in their excuse? And that's literally all it comes down to. Especially in this kind of sale, because it's high volume, it's not a complex sale. It's a one and done sale. This isn't a long sale cycle. Literally 1% of people who leave after that day come back. So, someone says then you think about it, they're gonna call you tomorrow, zero. Like, basically no chance. Like, and everyone has that person who they talk to and you're like, she's really gonna call back. And I'm like, my name, my girl's name was Leslie and I still, I'm still waiting for her call. All right, everyone's got one and you'll remember her or him. But anyways, belief is the core of the issue, okay? Now, energy. If you're really tired and you're down at the end of the day and you're like, don't wanna talk to anyone, people can feel that. And what they're buying from you initially is your energy. They're buying you. Like, they haven't even been to the gym, they haven't even worked out yet. They're just buying whether they like you, really. So, understand that the number one thing that you're gonna be doing is, sorry, there's too many things. One is that sales is a transfer of enthusiasm, all right? So, if you're not enthusiastic about the product, you can't transfer anything because you're not even starting with enthusiasm, right? So, you have to be enthusiastic, okay? The second thing is that the basic tenet of sales is that people will buy from you as long as they like you and they trust you. If they do not like you or they do not trust you, they will not buy from you, right? Because if they trust you, then they believe that what you're saying is actually gonna help them, right? And if they like you, they'll wanna buy it from you. So, I'll give you two examples. One is like a super boot campy, like, all right, motherfucker, like we're gonna lose weight, you're gonna sweat. Like, they might believe that that guy can get them their results, but they're like, I don't fucking like him, right? So, they don't wanna do business with him. Flip side, you have someone who's like, oh, I totally understand, you know, let's just do it at your own pace. You know, don't worry, if you miss a couple of workouts, you know, you cheat, we're all human. You might like that person, but they're not gonna fucking get you results, right? And so, there's a balance, you need both. And that's what the overarching objective is, is that if you can establish that they like you and that they can trust you, when you make the pitch, it's done, right? Either they don't like you or they didn't trust that what you're saying is true. That's it, it's the only reason people don't buy from you, okay? So, those are the overarching objectives. Now, what's a really good way to get people to like you? Have really high energy. So, understanding how to control your state of mind and your energy is super, super important, okay? So, for me, I do this thing called like a Bernie dance. So, I like, I'm like, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, so I do this dance and I have a really hard time not being in a good mood after I do that dance. So, whatever your thing is, maybe you need to listen to like, I don't know, Taylor Swift, like, whatever your thing is, it's not like a pump up song, like you get like ready to lift, like it has to be something that's goofy as shit and makes you laugh and makes you smile, makes you playful and makes you excited to see someone when they walk in the door. You can see the 19th person you've seen that day, right? It's you're their first person and they made all this time, they put in the schedule, they drove all the way to hear your pitch, right? To change their lives. So, just understanding that is huge, okay? So, managing your state, how much energy you have, having a little routine for that, every batter before they get up to bat, they have their own little routine that they do to get in the right state. Same exact thing with sales, they even call them at bats, right? So, manage your state, right? Second, you gotta believe in the product, like we covered. All right, you have to believe in it. If you don't believe in it, they can see it, they can smell it, all right? And what separates good salespeople from great salespeople is just consistency. Is do they close at 75% day in, day out? Or do they go on hot streaks, right? Hot streaks means that you can't control your state, that's all it is. So, someone's like, man, I just get hot and cold. And you hear that from a lot of salespeople. It's just my opinion, less experienced salespeople because like, I learned how to manage that because I always had to pay my bills. Like I had to pay payroll. So, I couldn't have cold streaks. I had to have hot streaks. So, you started, I started reverse engineering what my hot streaks were, and it was a series of actions, a series of behaviors, like I had to be in this mood, I had to be excited. I had to, like if I just had a, you know, someone just told me that they lost a bunch of weight, I was way more excited and I sold way more. So, I started thinking about those things, like, okay, so I need to read testimonials of people who've crashed the program. That's awesome. I need to do my little Bernie dance, so I'm excited, right? And then from the moment they walk in the door, gotta hit them in the face with a wow. All right, what I mean by that is like, someone wants to be like, hey, Susan! They're like, yeah, I'm like, happy Tuesday. You say that to someone, they're like, happy Tuesday to you too, right? I like this guy, already, I like this guy, right? I'm already building rapport, okay? So, like, we're taking it like, and we're taking the fight to them, right? We have so much told me, we have so much energy, we're so excited about this program. They have no, they're like, they're like, fuck, like, if this is the kind of energy that this place is about, like, I'm digging this, right? They had a bad day, whatever, you're gonna make it a good day, like, even if you don't sell them, just make their debt, right? And you make someone's day, not a 10-10-10, you're gonna sell them, right? So, that is the energy rapport, belief piece of this that I think is 75% of the sales coming down to this stuff. Obviously, the structure is fluid, we've done this a million times, and I can tell you exactly what questions to ask to lead to the sale. But having that right mindset, believing in the product, read the testimonial, see how many people whose lives have been changed by this. Report, wow them as soon as they come in the door, hey, happy Tuesday, how you doing? I'm so excited to see you pumped. All right, you ready to rock and roll? You ready to change your life? This is gonna be amazing, right? They're like, who is this guy, right? I used to literally do a cartwheel before I saw people, and they would see me do the cartwheel, they were like, what the heck is going on? But they'd laugh, and they were like, this guy's hilarious, like, this guy's, like yeah, I'll buy your shit. You know what I mean? Like, I like this guy, all right? And then obviously the energy piece. So report and energy kind of go hand in hand, because if you have overwhelming energy, you're super excited, you've managed your state, you have your burning dance, you listen to it, whatever it is to get in that state, they're gonna like you, they're gonna buy from you, and they're gonna believe you when you're saying, this is gonna change your life, all right? That is the front piece of the head game before we get into the clarification of the sale and all that kind of jazz, all right? Hopefully it makes sense. This is one of the two most important parts, bringing the pain is the other most important part, and this is the other one, okay? So that's it, and we're gonna get to the next step in the sale.
